description: Estimated AI compute reserves per company. GPU counts in thousands of H100-equivalent units. Power in Megawatts of data-center capacity.

companies.label: Microsoft Azure
companies.gpuNote: ~300k H100-class GPUs across global Azure AI fleet, including OpenAI-dedicated superclusters
companies.notes: Owns and operates data centers globally; co-designed Maia 100 AI accelerator

companies.label: Google
companies.gpuNote: ~280k H100-equivalent units plus proprietary TPU v5p fleet
companies.notes: Owns data centers and designs TPU ASICs in-house; partial H100 fleet for GCP customers

companies.label: AWS
companies.gpuNote: ~250k H100-class GPUs plus Trainium 2 and Inferentia 3 custom silicon
companies.notes: Owns data centers globally; AWS Trainium/Inferentia are proprietary AI chips

companies.label: NVIDIA
companies.gpuNote: ~35k H100/H200/B200 units used internally for R&D, DGX Cloud, and simulation
companies.notes: Designs its own silicon but relies on TSMC for fabrication; operates limited DGX Cloud infrastructure

companies.label: OpenAI
companies.gpuNote: ~25k H100-class GPUs allocated from Microsoft Azure dedicated capacity
companies.notes: Exclusively on Azure infrastructure; no owned data centers or custom silicon as of 2025

companies.label: Anthropic
companies.gpuNote: ~20k H100-class GPUs from AWS dedicated capacity
companies.notes: Primary infra on AWS; Google Cloud provides secondary compute via equity partnership

companies.label: Mistral
companies.gpuNote: ~5k H100-class GPUs via Microsoft Azure and NVIDIA partnerships
companies.notes: No owned infrastructure; relies on Azure for training and deployment at scale
